title: Mnesithea helferi
intro: Mnesithea helferi, also known as Helfer's grass, is a perennial grass species native to Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Mnesithea helferi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Mnesithea helferi is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Mnesithea helferi belongs to the family Poaceae, a large and diverse group of grasses. It is a type of grass, and its classification places it within the subfamily Panicoideae.
